name: Toyotomi Hideyoshi
description: Toyotomi Hideyoshi, a pivotal figure in Japanese history, rose from humble peasant origins to become one of the most powerful men in the archipelago. Born in Owari Province (present-day Aichi Prefecture), his early life is shrouded in some mystery, but he entered the service of Oda Nobunaga, quickly distinguishing himself through his cunning, bravery, and organizational skills. Nicknamed 'Kozaru' (little monkey) by Nobunaga, he proved instrumental in Nobunaga's campaigns to unify Japan. After Nobunaga's assassination in 1582, Hideyoshi swiftly avenged his lord and seized power, continuing Nobunaga's unification efforts. He implemented significant land surveys, standardized taxation, and restructured the social hierarchy, solidifying his control. While he lacked noble birth, he adopted the title of Kampaku (Imperial Regent) and later Taiko (retired Regent) to legitimize his rule. His ambitious foreign policy led to two disastrous invasions of Korea, which ultimately drained Japan's resources and weakened his dynasty. Despite the failures in Korea, Hideyoshi's internal reforms laid the groundwork for the stable Edo period under the Tokugawa shogunate, leaving a lasting impact on Japanese society and governance. He remains a complex and controversial figure, admired for his achievements and criticized for his ruthlessness and foreign policy blunders. His story is a testament to social mobility and the power of ambition in a period of great upheaval.
subtitle: The Peasant Who Unified Japan
achievements: Unified Japan after the Sengoku period
Implemented land surveys and standardized taxation

keyMyths.title: Hideyoshi's Rise from Peasant to Power
keyMyths.description: Hideyoshi's improbable ascent from humble origins to become the ruler of Japan is a central element of his legend. He achieved this through a combination of intelligence, ambition, and exceptional military and administrative skills, defying the rigid social structures of the time.

keyMyths.title: The Unification of Japan
keyMyths.description: Hideyoshi continued Oda Nobunaga's work to unify Japan, facing numerous powerful warlords. His military campaigns and strategic alliances eventually brought the country under his control, ending the long period of civil war.

keyMyths.title: The Korean Invasions
keyMyths.description: Driven by ambition, Hideyoshi launched two large-scale invasions of Korea, aiming to conquer the peninsula and potentially expand into China. These campaigns ultimately failed, resulting in significant losses and weakening his regime.

sources.author: Mary Elizabeth Berry
sources.description: A comprehensive biography exploring Hideyoshi's life and achievements.
sources.text: A comprehensive biography exploring Hideyoshi's life and achievements.
